category_title: Non-verbal male elementary students with classic autism learning phonics and letter recognition
minimal_context: The student is sitting face-to-face with the tutor using an iPad app to identify and match letters.
dialogue:
TUTOR: Let's use the iPad. Point to the letter on the screen to match it.

STUDENT: (Looks away from the iPad screen and stares directly at the tutor's face instead of the letters.)

TUTOR: Look at the letters on the screen.

STUDENT: (Continues to stare at the tutor, attempting to socialize by smiling at the tutor's face rather than looking at the visual app.)

TUTOR: Try to find the letter.

STUDENT: (Maintains intense eye contact with the tutor, completely ignoring the iPad app and its audio/text rewards, demonstrating the problematic socializing behavior that interferes with the visual learning task.)

options.A: Conduct functional behavior analyses targeting the socializing variables and subject them to data-based hypothesis testing.
options.B: Start the learning by introducing letter forms using a variety of concrete objects such as dice and cardboard.
options.C: Introduce two to five letters per month and ask the student to identify the letters at the end of the month.
options.D: Implement facilitative strategies and communication temptations to encourage the student to interact with the iPad.
